column1: # PII scrub manifest. Tab-separated: action <TAB> pattern <TAB> replacement <TAB> note
#   action = scrub       -> applied as a regex substitution during `build`
#            block-only  -> never substituted, but BLOCKS the ship if found in a
#                           non-exempt file during `scan` (defence in depth)
#            warn        -> surfaced in the review, never blocks (FP-prone)
# `pattern` is a Python regex (it is fed straight to re.compile). All rows are
# re-checked during `scan`: scrub+block-only must be 0 in non-exempt files, else
# the ship is blocked (a scrub gap is treated as a leak, fail-closed). Matches
# inside scrub-exempt paths (.mailmap) are downgraded to warnings.
# Footprint verified 2026-05-31 against the live working tree: personal Windows
# paths = 0 hits (already cleaned in place); private emails = .mailmap only
# (intentional, exempt); real name = ~7 shipping files; GCP id = 0 (kept as a
# regression guard). The scan double-checks regardless.
# 2026-07-03: the maintainer's real name scrub was RE-ACTIVATED (it had been
# commented out under a "name public" decision, which re-exposed the surname the
# v1.0.0 history was filter-repo-scrubbed of). Per explicit maintainer decision,
# the real surname "Lütke"/"Luetke" stays OUT of the public tree, consistent with
# v1.0.0. The public brand handle "@Ruben_Herz"/"Herz" remains intentionally
# public (see the Herz note at the end of this file) and is NOT scrubbed.
# --- maintainer GitHub login + entity slug (MUST precede the bare-name rules
# --- below, else the bare "ruben" rule would fragment them) -----------------
scrub
column4: maintainer github login (appears in a stream-evidence test fixture)

column1: scrub
column4: maintainer entity slug (= slugify of full name; keeps name->slug test couplings consistent)

column1: # --- maintainer real name (order matters: full name before surname) ---------
scrub
scrub
scrub
scrub
scrub
# --- maintainer bare first names: full-depersonalization (2026-06-01). Two
# --- distinct neutral names preserve router-prompt semantics where "Ruben" is
# --- the user (address) and "Harald" is an example third person mentioned. ---
scrub
scrub
column4: maintainer first name lowercase (config defaults, slugs, branch tags)

column1: # Accented spelling 'Rubén' — the ASCII 'Ruben' rules above do NOT match the é,
# so the accented real first name shipped through (2026-06-30 sub-agent review:
# docs/phase-b1-wiki-curator/*, tests/unit/memory/wiki/test_page.py, etc.).
scrub
scrub
scrub
column4: maintainer first name accented all-caps (regression guard)

column1: scrub
column4: maintainer alt first name (example person in prompts/tests)

column1: scrub
column4: maintainer alt first name lowercase (ADR owner field, slugs, test emails)

column1: # --- all-caps identifier residue: the case-sensitive scrub above misses
# --- SCREAMING_CASE used in JS test constants (e.g. HARALD_PAGE) and slug-fn
# --- test inputs (e.g. person_slug("RUBEN")). Preserve the all-caps style in
# --- the replacement so the depersonalized test stays internally consistent. -
scrub
column4: maintainer alt first name all-caps (JS test constants, e.g. HARALD_PAGE)

column1: scrub
column4: maintainer first name all-caps (slug-fn test input, e.g. person_slug("RUBEN"))

column1: scrub
column4: maintainer surname all-caps (regression guard; 0 current hits)

column1: scrub
# `.` matches the path separator on any OS, so no backslash escaping is needed.
# The `[\\/]+` rule below additionally catches ESCAPED / double-separator forms
# (e.g. "C:\\Users\\Administrator" in source string literals & docstrings) that
# the single-`.` pattern misses; it is a strict superset and runs as defence.
scrub
scrub
column4: windows personal home path (escaped/double-separator variant in source literals & docstrings)

column1: scrub
# --- cloud project identifier (regression guard; 0 current hits) ------------
scrub
# --- windows account SID (2026-06-19 privacy review) ------------------------
# The maintainer's machine/account SID leaked via scripts/drift-guard-task.xml
# (the -500 RID = builtin Administrator). Mask the unique machine-identifier
# prefix; any RID suffix is preserved so the XML stays SID-shaped as a template.
scrub
column4: windows account SID machine identifier (maintainer box)

column1: # --- private emails: never auto-edited (would break the .mailmap mechanism), --
# --- but BLOCK if they appear in any non-exempt shipping file ----------------
block-only
block-only
# --- any other consumer-mailbox address: surface for a human look -----------
warn

# tests), kept public on purpose. Do NOT re-add a Herz scrub — it would anonymize
# the maintainer's own chosen public project identity. (The real surname "Lütke"
# stays scrubbed above; "Herz" is the deliberately-public brand handle.)
